item.title: Let’s see what happens
item.description: Leaving this here to see how well it ages in the next 4 years. Prices from local market in Oregon FP Edit: Never gone viral before this is cool, thank you all! These prices are from our local family owned neighborhood market. It is undoubtedly cheaper to do the bulk of our family’s shopping at the chain stores but this place is close and convenient to pick up a few things . They also seem to be more sensitive to big price swings and thus might provide a more accurate measure of increasing costs compared to the big chain stores. I will try to update will weights if I get the time. Just wanted to have empirical evidence in case the mouth breathing red hats try to defend their messiah when he inevitably skyrockets prices.

item.title: Dumping Memes on a Sunday evening
item.description:
OMG these commercials are so annoying
Me, daily at work...
Me vs my wife
A lot of social media users need to understand the differences, 99% of you are not getting gaslighted.
Or being played in your local grocery store...
I have taken to calling people fornicators, it's not as rude as the other f word, but still gets people's attention.
"To me, it underscores our responsibility to deal more kindly with one another" - Carl Sagan
Gus Tax Enjoy your weekend Imgur, thank a veteran tomorrow!

greetings online friends
good movie with these 2 - the wrestler
try the and
gotta love that
mmm hmm
want no part of that at any time
as a guy... yeah, i've done this
yes
as is tradition
no idea
damn that's a nice bench
real mature, guy who made this
pengin
mortifying
and there you have it
lies
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: At least two of us are still friends with the DM...
item.description: The simple of it is that one of our members started posting 'haha funny Trump won' memes in our discord as well as other things implying that all lgbt folks are pedophiles. The DM banned him because they don't tolerate that kind of nonsense. Two other members jumped ship afterwards, one because he was friends with the first guy and had more fun in game with him, and the other because he thought he should be banned as well for believing that pedos were hiding amongst lgbt groups. So now our group doesn't have enough players to continue. The two of us that remain are still willing to keep going and are friendly with the DM. He said we'll try to pick things up in January but I'm not sure if we'll pick up new folks or not. I've only been in two D&D games in my whole life, the first one I ended up leaving because of group tensions and now this one is messed up too. *sigh* I don't fault the DM for what they did, I agree with it honestly. I'm just sad that this happened.
item.dc:creator: TalanRhodes

item.title: I keep thinking about the wreck of the FV Destination
item.description:
I keep thinking about this ship wreck. I wanted to share those thoughts with someone. But I also like to provide enough context that you don’t need to hop through other internet tabs to understand it. That made this a little lengthier than I initially intended. I tried to use memes to make the information less overwhelming. I also tagged “science” in this, because I cover the nature of the Bering Sea. . . I’ve never actually watched “The Deadliest Catch” until now. I stumbled upon a YouTube video about the FV Destination from the show. I have a thing for shipwrecks, so I wanted to learn more. Then I got sorta-hooked. For those unfamiliar with “The Deadliest Catch” it’s a reality tv/documentary series about the dangers of commercial fishing (particularly crabbing) on the Bering Sea. One episode on the show features the sudden sinking of the fishing vessel “The Destination.” I did some extensive research into the wreck - including reading two official reports from the coast guard and national transportation safety board. Now I can’t stop thinking about it. Before I get into the tragedy, there’s a little bit of context that I’d like to give, on crab fishing, starting with the nature of the Bering Sea.
I do want to make a note that the entire Bering Sea isn’t shallow. It’s broken into multiple sections. - A shallow underwater plain, with a depth of approximately 250ft (76.2m) - Two basins that exceed a depth of 12,000ft (3657.6m). - The Bowers Basin (deepest one), with a depth of 13,442ft (4,097m), The underwater plain is the relevant section of the sea, but it isn’t the only one. Moving on…
Icing is when the cold weather freezes the water that’s been splashed onto the ship, creating an accumulation of ice. The more ice there is, the heavier it becomes - and this isn’t just “a little bit of ice.” Icing can add literally thousands of extra pounds (or kilograms) of weight into a ship. If the ice gets too heavy, it can compromise the stability of the vessel for a few reasons, a big one being that it can basically create more weight than the ship is able to handle when riding massive waves in the middle of the ocean.
There’s not much to say on this, except that this place is volatile. My dumbass didn’t even know that “arctic hurricanes” were a thing until reading into the FV Destination.
We already covered the Bering Sea, so let’s move forward. . . HEAVY AND DANGEROUS EQUIPMENT Crabs are caught by massive crab cages (aka “crab pots”) that are loaded with bait. These cages can weigh up to 750 - 850 pounds (340 - 385.5 kilograms) when empty. When they’re full of crabs, they can weigh up to 1,000 pounds (453.5 kilograms). They are also big, with dimensions of 7ft by 7ft by 3ft (2m, 2m, 1m). These pots are handled by pulleys and other things, but also by humans. It only takes one hit in the head to kill someone. Todd Kochutin was a fisherman who died at the age of 30 after being hit in the head with an 800lb (362kg) crab pot. In addition to that crabbing is an EXTREMELY laborious task, which leads us to the next risk, exhaustion and physical exertion. . . EXHAUSTION AND PHYSICAL EXERTION Commercial crabbing is not the same as a steady 9 - 5 job. There’s only a window of time that fisheries will take crabs, and the fishers are paid by the amount of crabs they bring in (this is called a “quota”). This makes time a severely limited resource. There is no time to properly recuperate. Fishers can be working with the crab pots on the deck for as long as 12 hours, creating severe exhaustion. If too much strain is put on the body, it can lead to serious health risks. In fact, there have been multiple emergency situations on the Deadliest Catch that reflects this. . - Sig Hansen had his first heart attack at age 49. Since then, he’s had another heart attack and a mini-heart attack - Nick Mavar died of a heart attack at age 59 - Tony Lara died of a heart attack at age 50 - Phil Harris died of a stroke at age 53 - Keith Colburn had a mini stroke at the age of 61 (though it should be noted that it’s not uncommon for people to start suffering strokes in their 60’s) . Now there’s one last piece of context to cover.
There is a piece of equipment on ships called an emergency position-indicating radio beacon (EPIRB). It releases a signal to the coast guard if the vessel is beginning to sink. Some EPIRBs are automatic, others are manual. Because some EPIRBs are automatic, the crew might not even know that the signal was sent. This will be important later. . As a second note, keep in mind that a captain of a ship doesn’t mean that they OWN the ship. Typically in commercial vessels, the actual ship is owned by a businessperson, and the captain is the one that sails the ship. This was also true with the RMS Titanic, which was owned by Bruce Ismay, but sailed by Captain Edward Smith. The captain of the FV Destination was Jeff Hathaway, but the owner of the ship was someone else.
The FV Destination had an incoming deadline to deliver a quote of 191,000 pounds of crabs to the Trident Seafood Plant. I’m not entirely clear on whether the ship had met the quota and was delivering it, or if they planned to meet the quota on the way to the plant. Then, like is said in the image, the coast guard received a signal from the Destination’s EPIRB. They didn’t hear a mayday call from the captain, which was extra concerning. Then the vessel simply disappeared. When the coast guard flew out to the location, all they found was floating debris - which included a life ring and the EPIRB. A Good Samaritan fishing boat helped the coast guard retrieve the life ring and distress beacon to verify the identity of the vessel. They confirmed it was the FV Destination. All six people on the boat perished. Their remains were never recovered, and instead were declared “lost at sea.” The loss of the FV Destination was especially significant because it was the largest loss of life in commercial fishing in recent years.
Obviously, there was an immediate investigation into the situation. Officials wouldn’t find the sunken boat until several months later, but they were able to collect other evidence to try to figure out what happened. There were several factors to this horrible event, and some of them proved that it was entirely preventable.
These vessels were BOGGED DOWN by ice. Crew members had to go out and physically remove the ice to keep their respective vessel safely buoyant.
With everything that I know now, I would not have voluntarily gotten on that ship.
This part of the report suggests that the captain is to blame for that, but he didn’t own the ship. The coat guard’s report said that they felt the owner of the ship was responsible for that aspect, having failed to have the vessel inspected by a qualified expert in recent years.
There were so many crab pots on this fishing vessel, it looked like a (non-fishing) cargo ship.
This part of the incident is going to sound very similar to (though not exact) an incident that happened 105 years prior. The captain of the Destination made the choice to continue fishing despite the heavy accumulation of ice on the ship, ice warnings, and the insane amount of cargo on board. (A ship carrying on despite ice warnings. Sound familiar?) The Destination hadn’t met their quota of crab yet, and were scrambling to get that sorted before the deadline. This led to pushing ahead in an ill-advised situation. However, this becomes less sympathetic when it was revealed that there WAS an alternative plan to relieve stress on the situation, but the captain chose not to take this plan. I don’t mean to speak ill of the dead, especially when they were cherished by so many people. I think that love is perfectly valid. But it is also simply a fact that this man chose the most reckless option of meeting their quota. Good people can make terrible decisions, and this is one of those cases.
These were the only images of the actual wreck site that were available, and really the only thing it did was positively identify the vessel. The visual data didn’t provide much else insight into the wreck, to my understanding.
I had to make a quick disclaimer - just because *I* see a chaotic mess of colored dots, doesn’t mean that an expert can’t read this image and extract useful information from it.
Like I said - an expert can identify the dots as crab pots, while I’m over here seeing a newbie’s attempt of making a shipwreck on Blender.
This is what officials think happened. There was too much weight and not enough stability - and the ship just rolled over. . Note: This TikTok video is an animation that was taken from an official documentary about the sinking.
This is what I’m truly venting about: I’m bothered by how the investigation of this wreck was handled. They found the vessel resting at the bottom of the Bering Sea at a depth of 250 feet (76.2 meters), but they only produced a couple of blurry images of the vessel with a submersible camera, and a blurry sonar image of it on its port side. At the time, ocean currents prevented them from getting a closer look (totally understandable). But they never went back to investigate it further, and that’s driving me insane. I feel like we could learn a lot more about it if we revisited it. There have been more thorough investigations on other vessels, including the FV North Star (sank off the coast of Massachusetts with no casualties). Yes, Destination is in deeper waters, but last year we retrieved the remains of the submersible The Titan (OceanGate submersible) from the wreck of the RMS Titanic, which is both at an extreme depth of 12,500 feet (3,800 meters) and full of strong currents. I think this really bothers me because the investigation of the actual wreck site only is damn near difficult to actually see. It deserves more respect than that, especially since it was considered the biggest loss of life with fishing vessels in two decades. I hope scientists go back to the wreck one day and give it a proper look over. That being said, I could very well be unfair about this. The Bering Sea is a very dangerous place for a multitude of reasons. Perhaps it’s unreasonable and naive of me to expect a team of researchers to go down to the FV Discovery in the same way that a team dove to the RMS Titanic.
No matter who was at fault, whether the wreck site is revisited, or the openly acknowledged hazardous nature of commercial crabbing, six people died. I’m so sorry for your loss, to anyone who knew these men personally.
National Transportation Safety Board review of the sinking of the FV Destination: https://www.ntsb.gov/investigations/AccidentReports/Reports/MAB1814.pdf The Coast Guard’s review of the sinking, as well as a list of recommendations to prevent it from happening again: https://media.defense.gov/2019/Mar/03/2002095494/-1/-1/0/REPORT%20OF%20INVESTIGATION%20FISHING%20VESSEL%20DESTINATION.PDF The Bering Sea 1: https://angusadventures.com/adventurer-handbook/beringstrait/#:~:text=The%20Bering%20Sea%20is%20one,power%20than%20deep%20sea%20waves. The Bering Sea 2: https://kids.britannica.com/kids/article/Bering-Sea/346095#:~:text=The%20Bering%20Sea%20is%20one,May%20or%20June%20and%20October. Ice accumulation 1: https://www.dco.uscg.mil/Portals/9/DCO%20Documents/5p/CG-5PC/CG-CVC/CVC3/notice/flyers/25E-Icing.pdf Ice accumulation 2: https://www.ntsb.gov/Advocacy/safety-alerts/Documents/SA-074.pdf The dangers of commercial crabbing: https://www.alaskamaritimeinjurylawyers.com/blog/2022/07/the-dangers-of-crab-fishing-in-the-bering-sea/ Seattle Times Article 1: https://www.seattletimes.com/seattle-news/overloading-heavy-ice-and-an-open-hatch-coast-guard-details-what-sank-the-seattle-based-destination/ Seattle Times Article 2: https://www.seattletimes.com/seattle-news/no-return-final-voyage-crab-boat-destination-sinks-bering-sea/ “Deadliest Catch” video 1: https://youtu.be/qxAeJGXBlxU?si=3FnZ4Rbw_HDtvAy1 “Deadliest Catch” video 2: https://youtu.be/BBw1r7kIGWM?si=LZNTafvjhVFgyeLo Mini-Documentary on the sinking of the FV Destination: https://youtu.be/FE26BYr9j34?si=W9v8up23NvrLeQxl
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: Sitting on a dock watching dolphins = good, wholesome, family entertainment.
item.description: I loved the way the kid was so excited he started clapping. It's awesome little moments like this that can inspire people to grow up to be a marine biologist or something. All of the pics and videos I post are taken by me. For more OC nature vids check my profile or look for See Through Canoe Company on YouTube, FB & other social media sites.

item.title: Tall girl meta at the climbing gym
item.description: Source : https://youtube.com/shorts/PahIRfIT8mc Not the usual cosplay content that I post on Imgur, but I thought that was funny haha. I enjoy climbing a lot even though I am pretty weak (got diagnosed with malnutrition when I was a kid and struggled to build muscle mass my whole life), it's been helping me feel more confident in my strength and slowly build muscles mass. I also thought it would be cool to share that side of me too sometimes. Have a nice day everyone!
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: We don't deserve dogs
item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: I choose the bear
item.dc:creator: jrpsmith

item.title: Not giving up on this girl
item.description:
Foster kitten Pumpkin Pie has not been eating or drinking, and has been losing weight and vomiting. My bestie came over to help me with her tonight.
We’ve been trying high-calorie gel, but Punkin keeps spitting it out. And I’m not gonna lie, after losing her brother Turkey this morning, I was feeling a little fatalistic. But I didn’t want to give up.
So we mixed up some kitten formula with probiotics and syringe fed her, and…
She swallowed almost all of it! Here she is resting afterwards.
After she finished snuggling with Jamie, I gave her fluids and set her up on the heating pad. We’re not giving up on you, baby girl!
I took down the pen separating Lizzie from her adoptive siblings, and Sweet Potato Casserole and Butternut Squash immediately found the cat tree and snuggled in for a nap. Lizzie is in there somewhere, but their floof is hiding her. I’m headed to bed, goodnight Imgur!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays
